ops.body:
Shipped in cc-skills 2fc32523da63 (batch), corrected/completed through 2026-07-20. Central changes to the fleet docs pipeline (tools/gd-build):

1. **Hard cap**: `gd-build build` supervises the real build (`python -m gd_build _build`) in its own process group; default 300s wall-clock, `GD_BUILD_TIMEOUT=<secs>` overrides, `0` disables; exit 124 with the quarto/pandoc tree killed on expiry; external SIGTERM/SIGINT forwarded (exit 128+n). Effective fleet-wide immediately: CI pulls gd-build from cc-skills@main at build time.

2. **grouped-reference-pages patch** (gd_build/patches.py + grouped_reference.py): collapses a curated `reference:` into one anchored page per titled group; captain-hook ~296 reference pages → 13. Sidebar membership via a fragment-free group leaf — NOTE the original "config round-trip drops section-level hrefs" rationale was later disproven (section hrefs round-trip fine; they just don't confer membership); comments corrected in cc-skills c9528db37e1e.

**Collision incident**: the 2fc32523 batch was committed from stale worktrees and reverted 7 concurrent patches (fleet-css, type-annotations, etc.); re-landed forward as 0842157a by another session. Lesson: worktree-based lanes must rebase onto freshly-fetched origin/main at handoff.

**Introspection follow-up RESOLVED (2026-07-20)** — the "Dynamic introspection failed…retrying with static analysis" waste:
- **Corrected premise**: only 3 symbols were excluded (HookResponse, InlineTests, TCondition) — NOT 5. The two symbols actually failing dynamic introspection were still listed: **ToolCall** (re-export from cc_transcript) and **COMMENT_TYPES** (a frozenset constant), both raising griffe CyclicAliasError through capt-hook's PEP 562 lazy `__getattr__` facade. TCondition is a plain union assignment, not PEP 695 (old comment mislabeled it).
- **Per-item static-fallback patch: built, correct, DISCARDED** — paired A/B measured it ~5x SLOWER (Step-15 26m51s vs stock 5m22s, equal load). Inverted premise: the stock whole-reference fallback is the FAST path — static parsing is cheap, while completing a full dynamic pass executes capt-hook's heavy native/LLM deps for all 148 items. Patch + tests preserved as the attached static-fallback.patch.
- **Real fix: `dynamic: false` pinned** in capt-hook's great-docs config (captain-hook commit 1889bda3, via .claude/fragments/great-docs.yml/great-docs-local.fragment.yml). Since the fallback rewrote the config and rebuilt statically on EVERY run, deployed output was already static — pinning is byte-identical output (proven by diff + a two-run determinism control; the only diffs are pre-existing cli/*.qmd title-encoding nondeterminism, independent of the setting) while skipping the doomed partial-dynamic pass. Local Step-15 76s vs 264s (~3.5x, measured at higher load); CI build step 192s vs 195–215s post-restore baselines, log shows "Using static introspection mode", zero fallback lines. great-docs sanctions the setting (config.py: "False for packages with cyclic aliases").
- **Re-inclusion of the 3 excluded symbols: impossible** — great-docs cannot document griffe Kind.TYPE_ALIAS in EITHER introspection mode ("Cannot document object of kind: Kind.TYPE_ALIAS"); a renderer gap, not a dynamic-vs-static issue. They stay excluded; fragment comment now states the accurate reason.
- capt-hook uv.lock drift (pyproject required cc-transcript >=14.6.1, lock pinned 14.4.0) reconciled in the same commit via prek.

**Remaining known items** (user-descoped): old per-symbol reference URLs 404 (accepted); `fast_sidebar: true` no-op key — lives in cc-skills plugin/guides/yml/great-docs-fleet.yml:9 (fleet fragment), not capt-hook's local fragment.
